programmes.show_description: Joining Radio 5 Live.
Mark Stephen and Rachel Stewart with stories from the great outdoors.
The stories that matter across Scotland and the world.
Joe Hart, Jordan North, William Hanson and Rick Edwards chat to Steven Mill and Gary Innes as they kick-off the weekend with lots of laughs, great guests and timeless tunes.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
We're live at Murrayfield, where Scotland host France in their fourth Six Nations game of the tournament.
Sportsound brings you all the latest football news and commentary from Dens Park, where Dundee host Motherwell in the Scottish Premiership at 3pm.
We're live at Hampden Park where Scotland play Luxembourg in the Women's World Cup qualifiers.
Scottish Cup holders Aberdeen travel to Dunfermline to fight for a place in the semi-finals, with all the action live on Sportsound.
Roddy Hart with a mix of classic tracks and new discoveries.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
Martin Geissler and the team with the best of this weeks Scotcast and the most interesting news stories in Scotland.
An act of worship with Church of Scotland minister Rev Gillean Maclean in Inverness.
Two hours of music and conversation with a faith and ethical perspective. Taking the week's events to ask what they say about our values and beliefs.
News, discussion and interviews from politics and beyond.
The Scottish Cup quarter-finals continue on Sportsound with all the action from Rangers against Celtic.
The most petty and ill-informed football show on radio.
Join Gary as he shares your special messages and dedications and plays your favourite Scottish Music requests.
Jamie MacDougall presents a very special edition marking International Women's Day, featuring music from Evelyn Glennie, Mhairi Hall, Jess Gillam and Ailie Robertson.
Roddy Hart plays handpicked tracks from Scotland and beyond. Featuring Edinburgh's master of dramatic baritone and indie noir, Hamish Hawk.
Joining Radio 5 Live.
